answer: To publish a GitHub Pages site privately, you need to have an organization account, and your organization must use GitHub Enterprise Cloud. This feature is supported by GitHub Enterprise Cloud.
2000 minutes for GitHub Free, 3000 minutes for GitHub Pro, and 50,000 minutes for GitHub Enterprise Cloud, the difference between the highest and lowest values is 48,000 minutes.
Certain contributor, commit, and code frequency insights are only available for repositories that have fewer than 10,000 commits, affecting GitHub Free for organizations and GitHub Team plans.

Any usage beyond the included amounts is billed to your account for private repositories, while GitHub Actions usage is free for standard GitHub-hosted runners in public repositories, and for self-hosted runners.

GitHub Free includes two-factor authentication enforcement. GitHub Enterprise includes additional security features such as authentication with SAML single sign-on, access provisioning with SAML or SCIM, deployment protection rules with GitHub Actions for private or internal repositories, audit log streaming, and IP allow list, as well as GitHub Advanced Security products like GitHub Code Security and GitHub Secret Protection, which are also available in GitHub Team.
